author: @BurntKittyForge-pl8ol
text: Mr RMS, i'm really busy lately but sometimes i reply my thoughts and toss random personal tinker ideas but i had mentioned i was fascinated by the uses of clutches, many applications once atleast then worked on a magnetic airconditioner automotive clutch where a magnet winding controls compressor operation or drive so my point is now i scrapped a washing machine with a high carbon , maybe stainless , thick rod the middle plastic paddle with 4 fins bolts to so i was studying the clutch to reuse it and took the motor labeled amps and V and rpms , with a cool water pump too, my point the clutch pads grab and the paddle smacks bit the load slows it doen so it spins and then the clutch grabs and the plastic paddle smacks the water and dirty cloths and all cycles the same direction REALLY COOL UNEXPECTED PLACE THE GEOMETRY .....GOD BLESS YOU SIR I BETTER WORK IT GETS DARK EARLIER AND STUFF ....(edit: to clarify this design the clutch pads non stop grab and release based on the load making the motor speed decrease and then the motor speeds and the clutch pads grab again (over and over the same direction to make the paddle smack the water/c;othes)  it looks like , it has a cool gearbox too ...

author: @VeniceInventors
text: Wouldn't it be able to work in a straight line if the ribs depth was constant (even though the width decreases), and the ribs ended shortly before the pole of the spheres (allowing the opposite side to mesh while still being meshed on the other side)? Maybe that's what the one from the patent with the constant width aims to achieve? It looks like one "star" is recessed while the other is extruded.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah i suppose it would - you could always just stick magnets in the end too

text: One part is missing for 180 or 360 degree range?

text: I think by looking at it, there would be a variable speed difference depending on how it's positioned.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: pretty much the ratio is one to one how ever it is positioned mate

author: @roboraser770
text: I think the neutral position could also be used as clutch, if the gears had a flat cap and could be pushed parallel to their axis of rotation.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice idea mate - thanks for sharing

author: @livingladolcevita7318
text: Does it mean the gear ratio changes as the gears move to the top? if so could make a more smoother gear change, more fluid/ gradual.

replies.author: @roboraser770
replies.text: You could change the gear ratio by making one of the gears bigger or smaller than the other so that the two gears have a different number of cogs, but rotating the gears doesn't change the gear ratio, because it doesn't change the number of cogs that touch each other with each revolution.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no mate - you can think of the hemisphere as a lot of stacked gears all the same size - as you move them around the size of the gears change but the relationship remains the same - that is one to one

author: @scotttovey
text: Cool. It has a natural neutral position and a natural reverse gear built in.

replies.author: @roboraser770
replies.text: You are right about the neutral position, but turning the gears to their other side wouldn't reverse the rotational direction of the gears.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@roboraser770 
I just did a mental visualization, and I can see your point.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
​@roboraser770 
More visualization and I'm pretty sure it reverses rotation.

I need to see if I can do an animated mock up in FreeCAD.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I must be missing something - what you say sounds right to me - do that animated mock up and let me know will you?

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering
I should have just drawn it out on paper like I just did. Less complicated. 

It's no different than moving a flat from one side of the drive gear to the other side of the drive gear.

The driven gear would have to engage a third gear for it to go in reverse.

My visualization muscle needs exercising, it's weak.
